output.market_context: Macro is in "transition" with stable fed funds (3.64%), rising CPI, and weakening consumer sentiment (49.8) — favoring Tech and Health Care tilts. Market is in a strong AI-led tape with QQQ/XLK near overbought RSI, oil bouncing, and indices flirting with record highs amid US-Iran deal optimism.
themes: AI capex / semis parabolic (MU, SMCI, AVGO, NVDA)
Mag-7 insider distribution into strength (AMZN, AAPL, META)
Defensive/consumer staples breaking down (WMT, COST, XLP)
Energy oversold bounce on Mideast headlines (XLE)
Overbought tech regime — chase risk elevated

ranked_candidates.primary_thesis: AI franchise leader with fresh Computex catalyst (new RTX/Spark chip), strong earnings beat, and dominant news polarity — minor insider sell is noise vs. setup.
ranked_candidates.narrative: NVDA prints the cleanest cross-signal alignment: high news polarity (0.96), bull MA stack, recent earnings beat, and tech-favored macro regime. One small director sell (~$134k) is negligible. RSI 60 leaves room versus more stretched peers.

ranked_candidates.primary_thesis: Semi capex beneficiary breaking out with strong news flow and bull MA stack; no insider distribution flag.
ranked_candidates.narrative: AVGO scores high on technicals (composite 80, RSI 70, 20d breakout) and news polarity (0.80). Clean tape with no insider selling. Slight overbought risk but trend confirms AI capex theme.

ranked_candidates.primary_thesis: Best technical composite in the universe with earnings beat, NVDA-partnership tailwind, no insider sales — high-quality megacap leader.
ranked_candidates.narrative: MSFT shows MA-stack bull, RSI 74 (stretched but not extreme), 20d breakout, and constructive news (RTX Spark partnership). Earnings beat confirms fundamentals. Watch overbought.

ranked_candidates.primary_thesis: 34% earnings surprise, Mizuho target hike on agentic AI demand, parabolic momentum — but RSI 80.9 is mean-reversion risk.
ranked_candidates.narrative: SMCI has explosive earnings beat (+34.5%), strong news polarity (0.84), and 73% 1-month momentum. RSI 80.9 and 33% 1-week move flag chase risk. High-conviction theme, but size accordingly.

ranked_candidates.primary_thesis: Memory cycle parabolic with Raymond James target hike; high Reddit buzz + analyst upgrades, though +99% in a month is extreme.
ranked_candidates.narrative: MU is the most retail-buzzed name (26 mentions) with strong technical composite and supportive news. 99% 1-month move and RSI 80.5 mean any pullback could be sharp. Trend is undeniable but entry is poor.

ranked_candidates.primary_thesis: Tech sector ETF capturing the AI rally with macro tailwind, but RSI 82 is the most overbought reading in the universe.
ranked_candidates.narrative: XLK aligns with macro-favored Tech sector, with 20d breakout and strong news flow. RSI 82.4 is extreme — better entry on pullback. Diversified vehicle reduces single-name overbought risk.

ranked_candidates.primary_thesis: Earnings beat, AI-PC chip narrative tailwind from NVDA partnership news, 44% monthly momentum.
ranked_candidates.narrative: AMD benefits from NVDA Computex halo and beat earnings by 5.8%. RSI 74 stretched, no 20d breakout yet. Solid setup but pricier entry than 2 weeks ago.

ranked_candidates.primary_thesis: Dow ETF at breakout with constructive news flow and moderate RSI — broadest exposure with less overbought risk.
ranked_candidates.narrative: DIA combines 20d breakout with RSI 69 (less stretched than QQQ/XLK) and positive news polarity. A measured way to participate in record-highs tape.

ranked_candidates.primary_thesis: Nasdaq breakout with AI macro tailwind, but RSI 78.6 limits upside on a chase.
ranked_candidates.narrative: QQQ is the cleanest pure-AI beta vehicle. Macro favors Tech. RSI elevated; better as a hold than fresh long.

ranked_candidates.primary_thesis: Small-cap ETF with bull MA stack, RSI 62 (room to run), and high positive news polarity — laggard catch-up candidate.
ranked_candidates.narrative: IWM offers the least-stretched technical setup among index ETFs (RSI 62, no 20d breakout yet). High news polarity (0.87) and rate stability help small caps. Risk-on tilt if rally broadens.

ranked_candidates.primary_thesis: Broad index at record highs with 20d breakout but RSI 75 limits fresh entry conviction.
ranked_candidates.narrative: SPY is in a strong uptrend with bull MA stack. Some 'lost decade' headlines temper polarity. Adequate but not a standout.

ranked_candidates.primary_thesis: WWDC/Siri AI narrative is constructive but $15.5M director sale by Levinson is a quality flag we don't ignore.
ranked_candidates.narrative: AAPL has bull MA stack and strong news (polarity 0.89), but a single $15.5M director sell pre-WWDC is notable. Earnings beat modest (+3.5%). Net positive but insider tape capping conviction.

ranked_candidates.primary_thesis: Macro-favored healthcare sector with positive news polarity and RSI 51.6 leaving plenty of room.
ranked_candidates.narrative: XLV is macro-favored under the transition regime. Technicals are neutral-to-bullish without overbought concern. A defensive tilt that aligns with regime.

ranked_candidates.primary_thesis: Oversold energy bounce driven by Iran headlines and 7-day losing streak ending; contrarian setup.
ranked_candidates.narrative: XLE has the highest news polarity (0.86) but bear MA stack and weak technicals. This is a reflexive bounce, not a regime change. Speculative; size small.

ranked_candidates.primary_thesis: Earnings beat and 'boring dividend giant' news narrative, but mixed MA stack and negative 1w/1m momentum.
ranked_candidates.narrative: V combines strong news flow (0.87 polarity) with weak technicals (RSI 48, mixed stack). A potential mean-reversion setup but needs trend confirmation.

ranked_candidates.primary_thesis: Massive CEO Jassy selling cluster ($15.7M, 22 sells) is the dominant signal — overrides moderate news/earnings positives.
ranked_candidates.narrative: AMZN had a big earnings beat (+68%) but CEO Jassy unloaded heavily May 21. Technicals mixed, RSI 51, no breakout. Insider tape is the deciding factor here — fade strength.

ranked_candidates.primary_thesis: Consumer discretionary ETF with neutral technicals; consumer sentiment crash (-6.5%) is a headwind.
ranked_candidates.narrative: XLY shows mixed MA stack and falling consumer sentiment. News polarity OK but sector is conflicted with weakening consumer.

ranked_candidates.primary_thesis: Financials lagging with mixed MA stack and softer Monday sector action.
ranked_candidates.narrative: XLF is neutral on most factors. Yield curve stable but financials underperforming. Hold/avoid.

ranked_candidates.primary_thesis: Industrials flat with neutral RSI; lacks catalyst.
ranked_candidates.narrative: XLI shows mixed stack, RSI 50, near-zero momentum. No edge either direction.

ranked_candidates.primary_thesis: Materials neutral with positive news polarity but flat technicals.
ranked_candidates.narrative: XLB is range-bound. Decent news polarity but no momentum or macro tailwind.

ranked_candidates.primary_thesis: Macro-favored healthcare with earnings beat but recent price weakness (-2.5% 1w).
ranked_candidates.narrative: UNH had a 9.5% earnings beat and macro favors healthcare, but technicals are softening. Setup is okay but momentum has paused.

ranked_candidates.primary_thesis: Strong earnings (+94% surprise) and AI capex theme offset by negative news polarity (-0.42) on debt-fueled AI concerns.
ranked_candidates.narrative: GOOGL's news flow has turned negative on 'Mag-7 hooked on debt' narrative. Technical mixed (RSI 51). Earnings strong but tape isn't confirming. Conflict.

ranked_candidates.primary_thesis: Bear MA stack, CLO/COO insider sells totaling $2.2M, despite positive news polarity — signals diverge negatively.
ranked_candidates.narrative: META has bear MA stack, RSI 43, and cluster sells from Chief Legal Officer and COO ($2.16M). News polarity high but the tape and insiders disagree. Avoid.

ranked_candidates.primary_thesis: Earnings miss (-36.5%), negative news polarity (-0.57) on OpenAI Robotics competitive threat — multiple red flags.
ranked_candidates.narrative: TSLA missed earnings badly and faces fresh competitive narrative from OpenAI Robotics. News polarity is the worst in the universe. Avoid.

ranked_candidates.primary_thesis: Post-Buffett era kicked off with poorly-received Taylor Morrison acquisition; bear momentum, RSI 39.
ranked_candidates.narrative: BRK.B has bear momentum (-3.7% 1w) and negative news polarity (-0.38) on the homebuilder acquisition. Earnings beat doesn't offset. Wait for stabilization.

ranked_candidates.primary_thesis: Consumer staples breaking down with falling consumer sentiment; not defensive enough to offset.
ranked_candidates.narrative: XLP RSI 38, mixed stack, -3.4% week. Despite defensive positioning, the move is wrong-way. Avoid until base forms.

ranked_candidates.primary_thesis: Bear MA stack and weak momentum despite earnings beat and high news polarity — price action diverging from headlines.
ranked_candidates.narrative: JPM shows bear stack, RSI 43, and -5.4% monthly momentum. News tape strong (0.86 polarity) but price won't confirm. Conflict — wait.

ranked_candidates.primary_thesis: In-line earnings disappointed; RSI 32, -8% 1-week capitulation despite positive news polarity.
ranked_candidates.narrative: COST missed expectations slightly and dropped 8% in a week. RSI 32 is oversold but trend is broken (price < MA20, MA50). Wait for base.

ranked_candidates.primary_thesis: Walton family + execs unloaded $313M in shares — massive insider distribution overrides earnings beat and positive news.
ranked_candidates.narrative: WMT had a small earnings beat but Walton Family Holdings Trust dumped >$130M plus CEO McMillon and EVPs adding. RSI 27, -13% month. This is the strongest insider sell signal in the universe — avoid.

ranked_candidates.primary_thesis: Worst technical composite (29) — bear stack, RSI 32, -7.8% month; rate-sensitive sector pressured.
ranked_candidates.narrative: XLU is in clear downtrend despite some positive news flow on data center demand. Wait for technical stabilization.

output.summary: Two weeks live: cold-start book lagged SPY by ~157bps in week 1, then the AI/semis tilt added ~175bps of alpha in week 2 for cumulative alpha roughly flat. Picks scoreboard shows GOOGL (-3.5%) as the worst drag and AMZN as the best — ironically we then trimmed AMZN.
what_worked: Doubling down on AI/semis on 5/25 (NVDA add, SMCI/MU/QQQ opens) coincided with +2.53% book vs SPY +0.78% — the high-conviction concentrated tilt paid in week 2.
Avoiding insider-flagged names (AAPL, MSFT, META, V, JPM, AMD) on cold start was disciplined and prevented additional drag.
Energy diversifier (XLE) held as macro tilt — survived NVDA-week binary without forcing a defensive posture.
what_missed: GOOGL (-3.5%) was the worst pick despite 'clean insider signal' framing — mega-cap AI proxies are not free alpha; news/technical didn't save it.
Trimmed AMZN 50% on 5/25 right after it was our best performer (+0.55%) — chased rotation into semis rather than letting a working medium-conviction position run.
Week-1 diversified cold-start lagged SPY by 157bps; 'spread small across 5 themes' was too defensive given the actual regime favored concentration.
UNH 'contrarian bounce' kept on 5/25 with explicit ballast-drag warning acknowledged — small position size limits damage but thesis quality is thin.
lessons_for_pm: Don't trim winners to fund theme rotations — AMZN was the only positive pick and got cut 50% to feed semis. Recycle from losers/flat names, not from what's working.
'Clean insider signal' on a mega-cap (GOOGL) is not a thesis by itself — require at least 2 confirming factors (technical + earnings or sentiment) before sizing ≥7%.
When regime is clearly trending (AI capex super-cycle), concentrated single-name expression beats broad diversification — week 2 confirmed this vs week 1's spread-thin approach.
Re-examine UNH each week: contrarian holds with acknowledged 'ballast drag' need an exit trigger, not indefinite hold.
High-conviction sizing worked in week 2; keep the discipline but pair every high-conviction add with a defined invalidation level (e.g., NVDA below technical pivot).
watch_for: Semis crowding — NVDA/SMCI/MU/QQQ all express the same factor; a single AI-capex headline reversal hits the whole sleeve.
GOOGL drawdown extension — if it doesn't stabilize this week, the 'mega-cap AI exposure' thesis needs to be cut, not averaged.
SPY beta creep: after week-2 adds, book is likely high-beta tech-tilted; check whether XLE/BRK.B/UNH ballast is still sufficient for any macro shock.

output.summary: Two weeks live: cold-start book lagged SPY by ~157bps in week 1, then the AI/semis tilt added ~175bps of alpha in week 2 for cumulative alpha roughly flat. Picks scoreboard shows GOOGL (-3.5%) as the worst drag and AMZN as the best — ironically we then trimmed AMZN.
what_worked: Doubling down on AI/semis on 5/25 (NVDA add, SMCI/MU/QQQ opens) coincided with +2.53% book vs SPY +0.78% — the high-conviction concentrated tilt paid in week 2.
Avoiding insider-flagged names (AAPL, MSFT, META, V, JPM, AMD) on cold start was disciplined and prevented additional drag.
Energy diversifier (XLE) held as macro tilt — survived NVDA-week binary without forcing a defensive posture.
what_missed: GOOGL (-3.5%) was the worst pick despite 'clean insider signal' framing — mega-cap AI proxies are not free alpha; news/technical didn't save it.
Trimmed AMZN 50% on 5/25 right after it was our best performer (+0.55%) — chased rotation into semis rather than letting a working medium-conviction position run.
Week-1 diversified cold-start lagged SPY by 157bps; 'spread small across 5 themes' was too defensive given the actual regime favored concentration.
UNH 'contrarian bounce' kept on 5/25 with explicit ballast-drag warning acknowledged — small position size limits damage but thesis quality is thin.
lessons_for_pm: Don't trim winners to fund theme rotations — AMZN was the only positive pick and got cut 50% to feed semis. Recycle from losers/flat names, not from what's working.
'Clean insider signal' on a mega-cap (GOOGL) is not a thesis by itself — require at least 2 confirming factors (technical + earnings or sentiment) before sizing ≥7%.
When regime is clearly trending (AI capex super-cycle), concentrated single-name expression beats broad diversification — week 2 confirmed this vs week 1's spread-thin approach.
Re-examine UNH each week: contrarian holds with acknowledged 'ballast drag' need an exit trigger, not indefinite hold.
High-conviction sizing worked in week 2; keep the discipline but pair every high-conviction add with a defined invalidation level (e.g., NVDA below technical pivot).
watch_for: Semis crowding — NVDA/SMCI/MU/QQQ all express the same factor; a single AI-capex headline reversal hits the whole sleeve.
GOOGL drawdown extension — if it doesn't stabilize this week, the 'mega-cap AI exposure' thesis needs to be cut, not averaged.
SPY beta creep: after week-2 adds, book is likely high-beta tech-tilted; check whether XLE/BRK.B/UNH ballast is still sufficient for any macro shock.

output.weekly_thesis: Recycle losers and weak-thesis names (AMZN insider dump, GOOGL negative tape, BRK.B/XLE drift) into a higher-quality tech sleeve via MSFT and AVGO — diversifies the semis-crowded book without chasing the most overbought names.
output.narrative: The semis sleeve (NVDA/SMCI/MU/QQQ) is already crowded and overbought — I'm not adding to the parabolas. Instead, I'm cleaning house: closing AMZN (CEO cluster sell dominates), GOOGL (negative news polarity, TLH benefit, reflection watch-list trigger), BRK.B (post-Buffett weakness, TLH benefit), and XLE (bear stack, TLH benefit) — all per the lesson to recycle from losers/flat names, not winners. Proceeds fund MSFT (highest technical composite, clean insider tape, lower vol) and AVGO (clean AI-capex breakout, no insider distribution) which diversify the AI-capex expression while raising sleeve quality. UNH stays one more week on earnings beat + macro fit but is on a tight leash.
